title: Insurtech Root Finishes 2024 With First Yearly Profit
text: Root Inc., the Columbus, Ohio-based parent company of Root Insurance, achieved a historic milestone by posting its first profitable year in company history during 2024. The insurer reported fourth quarter net income of $22.1 million, compared to a $24 million loss in the same period the previous year. For the full year 2024, Root earned $30.9 million in net profit, a dramatic turnaround from a net loss exceeding $147 million in 2023. The company's net combined ratio for Q4 improved to 91.5, down from approximately 112 in Q4 2023. CEO Alex Timm attributed the success to the company's technological capabilities, stating: Our proprietary technology has propelled us to net income profitability with significant room for growth. Root expanded its policy base by 21% in 2024, reaching 414,862 total policies. Gross premiums written surged 66% to $1.3 billion. The company currently operates in 35 states with additional filings pending, and recently expanded into Minnesota. The partnership channel, including Caravan Insurance and Goosehead Insurance, experienced remarkable growth of 115%, accounting for nearly one-third of new fourth-quarter writings. Timm emphasized the company remains laser-focused on disciplined underwriting driven by technology and algorithms. He noted Root has implemented modest rate decreases for qualified drivers in select states while avoiding aggressive pricing strategies aimed solely at market expansion. Policies obtained through partnership and independent channels demonstrate longer retention and higher average values. Regarding potential economic disruptions like tariffs, Timm expressed confidence in Root's technological infrastructure, stating: Being on a tech chassis actually positions you better than a lot of our incumbent competitors.
